161.description: California Botanical Gin aged 6 months in American oak. Organic corn base with shiso, pistachios, Meyer lemon, nasturtium. 93pts Wine Enthusiast
161.name: Jettywave Barrel-Aged Botanical Gin

162.description: California Botanical Gin from Half Moon Bay. Made with local botanicals from distillery garden
162.name: Jettywave California Botanical Gin

163.description: New York Style gin inspired by KISS song 'Cold Gin'. 5x distilled grain base with juniper, lemon, herbs. No sweeteners
163.name: Kiss Cold Gin New York Style

164.description: Barrel-aged gin pioneered in 2005. Made from 88% wheat vodka, 12% grape eau de vie, aged in French oak. Gold 2019 World Gin Awards
164.name: Old World Spirits Rusty Blade Gin

166.description: Gin made with botanicals from Angel Island, San Francisco Bay
166.name: Seven Stills Wood Isle Dry Gin

513.description: A refined dry gin from Umbria, Italy. Opens with bright citrus and Italian juniper, followed by floral ginestra and green olive leaf. Subtle warming spice from peperoncino and coriander, finishing dry, herbaceous, and complex. Botanicals: Italian juniper, olive leaf, coriander, ginestra, orange, peperoncino. Triple Gold Medal at MicroLiquor Spirit Awards.
513.name: Quattro Gatti Gin Classico

514.description: A dry gin distilled with olive leaf, olives, and olive oil from ancient Italian groves. Earthy, elegant, and quietly complex — crafted as the ultimate martini gin with savory depth and structure. Opens with forest floor and rain-soaked stone, round juniper presence with velvety mouthfeel from olive oil. Botanicals: Italian juniper, olive, olive leaf, olive oil, artichoke, coriander, lemon peel, thyme. Gold Medal at San Francisco World Spirits Competition and World Gin Awards.
514.name: Quattro Gatti Olive Grove Gin

519.description: A savoury, earthy gin created by James May celebrating two staples of the California hippie movement: mushrooms and patchouli. Girolle mushrooms provide a light, fruity, peppery note with a hint of apricot, paired with delicate earthy woody patchouli. Oregano enhances the savoury herbaceous notes while balancing coriander and lemon peel. Winner of England's Signature Botanical category at the 2025 World Gin Awards.
519.name: James Gin California Dreamgin'

520.description: A savoury gin created by James May that distils the essential flavours of Americana — hot-dog mustard and dill pickles. Light and warming with a slight sensation of fire on the tongue. Built around yellow mustard seed and gherkin, with juniper, coriander, angelica root, orange peel, bay, caraway, ginger, dried tomato, rosemary, cubeb pepper, and licorice root. Originally released as "American Ramstud."
520.name: James Gin American Mustard

554.description: Gin with juniper, mountain sage, and nine botanicals by Spirits of the Wasatch, capturing Utah&amp;amp;#039;s high-mountain desert. Earthy, piney, sage-forward with white pepper and rosemary. Gold Medal Bartender Spirits Awards.
554.name: High Mountain Desert Gin

560.description: Distinctive gin distilled from upcycled Grand Cru grape skins from Domaine Watson&#039;s vineyard in Chablis, Burgundy. Cultivated in France, distilled in England. Botanicals include juniper, lemon peel, coriander, linden flowers, cubeb berries, acacia honey, angelica root, lime, and Kimmeridgian stone. Co-founded by Emma and Alex Watson, drawing on their family&#039;s 30+ year winemaking heritage. Gold Medal, Spirits Business Gin Masters 2023.
560.name: Renais Gin

575.description: Award-winning Western Style gin from West Texas featuring hand-foraged wild Red Berry Juniper from the West Texas Mountains. Botanicals include organic common juniper, coriander, orange peel, lemon peel, Centennial hops, cinnamon, angelica, orris root, lemon balm, and white pepper. Bold and complex with aromas of vanilla, caramel, and rye. Gold Medal, 2018 San Francisco World Spirits Competition.
575.name: WildJune Western Style Gin

576.description: West Texas dry gin featuring hand-foraged wild Alligator Juniper berries with a rye and malted barley spirit base. Botanicals include organic common juniper, orange peel, orris root, and coriander. Earthy juniper upfront with a pop of citrus from orange peel on the mid-palate. Clean and versatile for Martinis, G&Ts, and classic gin cocktails.
576.name: WildBark West Texas Dry Gin

617.description: A Utah gin from Spirits of the Wasatch aged at least 8 months in used rye whiskey barrels, developing a soft golden color with juniper and mountain sage character alongside barrel-derived sweetness. Nose: sage, pine, caramel, vanilla. Palate: caramel, vanilla, allspice, sage, pine. Finish: vanilla, baking spice, sage, white pepper. Works well in cocktails or served neat over ice.
617.name: High Mountain Desert Barrel Rested Gin

621.description:
James May&#039;s petrichor-themed gin from the James Gin lineup (Wiltshire, England). Named for the wet weather assumption that haunts London, the botanical bill is built around recreating the smell of rain hitting warm pavement — a quality that comes specifically from geosmin, which is also present in beetroot.

Botanicals: juniper, citrus peels, coriander, angelica root joined by beetroot, salt, lovage, ginger, and bay. The result is a savory, earthy, almost umami gin — quite distinct from a classic London Dry. Tasting notes describe rich juniper and tangy orange up front, then earthy beetroot and rooty botanical spice, with mineral petrichor and a peppercorn warmth on the finish.

40% ABV. Cocktail applications: lean into the savory side — Negronis where the earthy quality plays against Campari&#039;s bitter (especially interesting with Cappelletti Pasubio for a smoke/earth interaction); martinis with celery bitters or a brined olive; gin-and-tonic with a tomato and basil garnish or a beet shrub. Less interesting in citrus-forward shaken drinks where the earthy quality fights with the brightness.

Pairs interestingly side-by-side with American Mustard from the same James Gin range — both are savory gins but pulling in different directions (mustard/spice vs. earth/petrichor). Good isolation-principle candidate for a &quot;savory gin&quot; flight alongside Quattro Gatti Olive Grove and Vicario Olive Leaf.
621.name: James Gin London Drizzle

622.description:
Limited-edition gin from Pacific House Gin (San Francisco) released September 2025 in partnership with Bay FC, the NWSL women&#039;s professional soccer franchise. Distilled by Master Distiller Montgomery Paulsen, the gin honors the California poppy — the state flower and a symbol of Bay Area resilience — and is the signature spirit in Bay FC&#039;s &quot;Pacific Poppy&quot; match-day cocktail at PayPal Park. Each bottle ships with an iron-on Bay FC x Pacific House patch.

43% ABV, grain-distilled. Botanicals lead with juniper, orange peel, and California poppy (including poppy seed for a creamy textural note), with coastal minerality and subtle spice. Nose: fresh juniper, bright orange peel, floral poppy, coastal mineral notes. Palate: citrus-forward brightness, creamy poppy-seed texture, soft spice, herbal lift. Finish: clean and gently floral.

Cocktail applications: the brand&#039;s house cocktail is the Pacific Poppy (gin + Aperol + elderflower liqueur + lemon, shaken, coupe) — essentially a gin-based Paper Plane riff. The bright citrus/floral profile also suits a Bee&#039;s Knees, French 75, Aviation (the floral element works with the violet), or a Last Word variant. Less ideal for Negroni territory where you&#039;d want more juniper backbone — Ford&#039;s, Plymouth, or Four Pillars are still better workhorses there.

Pairs as a comparative California gin alongside Jettywave California Botanical, James Gin California Dreamgin&#039;, and Freeland Forest Gin for a &quot;California terroir&quot; flight — each takes a different angle on the regional botanical theme.
622.name: Pacific House Bay FC Poppy

625.description: Western-style gin from Salida, Colorado. Medium juniper-forward with Colorado-grown organic Cascade hops, elderflower, and other botanicals. Hops contribute floral and citrus character without bitterness — distinctly NOT an IPA-style gin. Useful for the &quot;hops as botanical&quot; comparison against Seven Stills hop-forward distillates (IPW, Five Pounds, Still Waldos) where hops are the structural base rather than a botanical accent.
625.name: Wood's Mountain Hopped Gin

636.description: Captive Spirits' flagship Big Gin (London Dry, Seattle WA) rested ~4 months in formerly-peated single malt barrels (Westland; Ardbeg/Laphroaig casks also referenced). Botanical bill: juniper, coriander, bitter orange peel, grains of paradise, angelica, cassia, orris, cardamom, Tasmanian pepperberry. Profile: understated-but-present peat — light smoke, menthol, ginger — with juniper and cracked black pepper on the palate, caramel/vanilla on the finish. Cask treatment dulls the citrus. Fills the barrel-aged/smoky gin gap; excellent for smoky Negroni and gin Boulevardier builds, and a clean isolation test against Ford's London Dry. 47% ABV / 94 proof.
636.name: Big Gin Peat Barreled
